label: Normal chest CT
report:
CLINICAL INDICATION: Routine screening, no acute symptoms.

TECHNIQUE: Axial CT images of the chest were obtained with intravenous contrast enhancement.

FINDINGS:
Lungs: The lungs are clear bilaterally. No focal consolidation, pleural effusion, or pneumothorax is identified. No pulmonary nodules are detected. The pulmonary vasculature is unremarkable.
Mediastinum: The mediastinum is midline without widening. No mediastinal lymphadenopathy is present. The heart size is within normal limits. No pericardial effusion.
Pleura: No pleural effusion or pleural thickening.
Bones: The visualised osseous structures are intact with no acute fractures.

IMPRESSION:
1. No acute cardiopulmonary process identified.
2. No pulmonary nodules or masses detected.
3. Normal study.
notes: Baseline normal report used to verify low-risk classification.

label: Pulmonary nodule with follow-up recommendation
report:
CLINICAL INDICATION: Cough and haemoptysis, 57-year-old male, 30 pack-year smoking history.

TECHNIQUE: Helical CT of the chest without intravenous contrast.

FINDINGS:
Lungs: There is a 12 mm spiculated solid nodule in the right upper lobe (axial image 34, series 3). Mild centrilobular emphysema is noted bilaterally, more pronounced in the upper lobes. No pleural effusion. No pneumothorax.
Mediastinum: There is mild enlargement of the right hilar lymph nodes, the largest measuring 1.3 cm in short axis. The cardiac silhouette is normal in size. No pericardial effusion.
Pleura: Mild bilateral pleural thickening without effusion.
Bones: Degenerative changes of the thoracic spine. No lytic or blastic lesions.

IMPRESSION:
1. 12 mm spiculated right upper lobe nodule with ipsilateral hilar lymphadenopathy. This is suspicious for primary lung malignancy. PET-CT and tissue sampling are strongly recommended.
2. Mild centrilobular emphysema, consistent with smoking-related change.
3. Follow-up per multidisciplinary tumour board recommendation.
notes: High-risk nodule case. Tests malignancy keyword detection and risk classification.

label: 간 낭종 및 신장 결석 (Korean source)
report:
임상 정보: 우상복부 통증, 44세 여성.

검사 기법: 조영제를 사용한 복부 및 골반 CT 촬영(3단계: 비조영, 동맥기, 문맥기).

소견:
간: 간 6분절에 직경 2.1 cm의 단순 낭종이 관찰됩니다. 낭종은 얇은 벽과 균일한 액체 음영을 보이며 악성 소견은 없습니다. 그 외 간실질의 미만성 이상 소견은 없으며 간내 담관 확장도 관찰되지 않습니다.
담낭/담도: 담낭에 다발성 담석이 보이며 가장 큰 것은 8 mm입니다. 급성 담낭염을 시사하는 주변 지방 침윤이나 담낭벽 비후는 없습니다.
비장: 정상 크기 및 음영.
췌장: 정상 형태 및 음영. 췌장관 확장 없음.
신장: 우측 신장에 5 mm 비폐색성 결석이 관찰됩니다. 신우 확장 또는 수신증은 없습니다. 좌측 신장은 정상.
대동맥/혈관: 복부 대동맥의 직경은 정상 범위입니다.
림프절: 후복막 림프절 종대 없음.

결론:
1. 간 6분절 단순 낭종 (2.1 cm), 양성 소견.
2. 담낭 담석증, 급성 담낭염 소견 없음.
3. 우측 신장 비폐색성 결석 (5 mm).
4. 그 외 복부 장기 이상 소견 없음.
notes: Korean source report. Tests KO→KO pass-through or KO→EN→KO pipeline depending on config.

label: Acute ischaemic stroke – critical finding
report:
CLINICAL INDICATION: Sudden onset left-sided weakness and speech difficulty, 68-year-old female.

TECHNIQUE: MRI brain with diffusion-weighted imaging (DWI), FLAIR, T1, T2, and gradient-echo sequences. No gadolinium administered.

FINDINGS:
Diffusion-weighted imaging: There is restricted diffusion in the right middle cerebral artery (MCA) territory involving the right insular cortex, right inferior frontal gyrus, and right temporal lobe. The ADC map confirms true restricted diffusion consistent with acute ischaemia.
FLAIR: Corresponding FLAIR hyperintensity is beginning to evolve in the right MCA territory, suggesting the infarct is likely 6–12 hours old.
Gradient echo: No evidence of haemorrhagic transformation.
T1/T2: No mass lesion. No midline shift. Ventricles are normal in size and configuration.
Extra-axial spaces: No subdural or epidural collection.
Small vessel disease: Mild periventricular and subcortical white matter changes, age-appropriate.

IMPRESSION:
1. ACUTE ISCHAEMIC STROKE in the right MCA territory. Immediate neurology consultation and consideration of thrombolytic therapy or mechanical thrombectomy is strongly recommended.
2. No haemorrhagic transformation identified.
3. Mild chronic small vessel ischaemic changes.
notes: Critical finding case. Tests critical_findings extraction and urgent alert pathway.

label: Liver metastases with primary colon cancer
report:
CLINICAL INDICATION: Known sigmoid colon adenocarcinoma, staging CT.

TECHNIQUE: Triple-phase CT abdomen and pelvis with intravenous contrast.

FINDINGS:
Liver: Multiple hypodense lesions are identified throughout both hepatic lobes. The dominant lesion measures 4.7 x 3.9 cm in hepatic segment VIII. At least 6 additional lesions are identified, ranging from 0.8 to 2.4 cm. The morphology and enhancement pattern are consistent with metastatic disease.
Gallbladder: Normal. No cholelithiasis.
Pancreas: Normal morphology and enhancement. No pancreatic ductal dilatation.
Spleen: Normal size and attenuation.
Kidneys: Bilateral kidneys enhance normally. No hydronephrosis or suspicious lesion.
Colon/Rectosigmoid: There is mural thickening and irregular luminal narrowing at the sigmoid colon measuring 5.2 cm in length, consistent with the known primary carcinoma. Pericolic fat stranding is noted. No free perforation.
Lymph nodes: Enlarged mesenteric lymph nodes (up to 1.8 cm) adjacent to the sigmoid lesion, suspicious for regional nodal metastases. No distant lymphadenopathy above the diaphragm.
Ascites: Mild free fluid in the pelvis.

IMPRESSION:
1. Multiple bilateral hepatic metastases, largest 4.7 cm in segment VIII.
2. Known sigmoid colon primary carcinoma with pericolic invasion.
3. Suspicious regional mesenteric lymph node metastases.
4. Small volume pelvic ascites, may represent peritoneal seeding.
5. Clinical staging: cT3 N1 M1 (hepatic metastases). Multidisciplinary oncology team review recommended.
notes: Metastatic disease case with complex multi-organ findings. Tests multi-sentence translation fidelity.
